ab: kahiwan iaku inai Baraubaw tu langqinunayuan, minalhupishiza iaku, mungqthaiza iamin mutusi Tai-paak, miaqay iamin ininthawan isa mawalhnaq a pruq.
ch: 小時候我們家住在日月村，上學以後，就搬到了台北，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Baraubaw. After I started school, we moved to Taipei and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: numa minalhupishiza sa mara’in a lhupilhupishan, lhminupish iaku minfazaq miniahala inai a thau a kazakazash a minfazaq a patashan, mafazaqiza iaku Tai-waan miniahala inai a thau a qbit itia manasha wa numanuma wa kazakazash.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: numawan, thuini a pinangqaiza mahnariza wa qali, makunakataunan iaku numa naak a tantuqash ayuzi a minlhafut numa makikalhi iamin tanatuqash, mangqtu iamin minfazaq sa naam a Thau a kazakazash.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: haya tuqatuqash makashpashpatiza wa qamishan, numa riqazan sundaiza parhawaywan.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: makikalhi iaku thithu mafazaq thithu numanuma wa lalawa, mathuaw thithu malhinuna Thau a lalawa mathuaw ma’ania, mafazaq mani thithu mashbunun a lalawa, numa Shpuut a lalawa numa Lit-puun a lalawa.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的邵語說得很流利，也會說布農語、閩南話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Thau, as well as Bunun, Hokkien and Japanese.

ab: makikalhi iaku thithu, numa mafazaq iaku, maqa thithu minalhupish Lit-puun a lalawa tu azazakuan, tu Bunun a thau thithu a binanau’az, numa mathuaw thithu tu malhkakrikriw mathuaw kafazaqin thithu manasha Shpuut, numawan mafazaq thithu manasha antu tataal a lalawa.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是布農族人，而他工作上認識許多閩南人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Bunu, and he knows many Hokkien at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: musuun iamin malhalhinuna, numa izai tanatuqash fuilh manasha muribush a kazakazash.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: sharaqian antu shiruan iamin mafazaq sa mulhthkiz a lalawa, kanuniza pulaunin sa bahi iamin pasain sa ling-waa, shduu iamin muqtha tmaza.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: maura iaku mita wa Thau a kazakazash tu azazakuan, mayaw iaku mzai Thau mita.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: mafazaqiza iaku thuini Thau a numanuma wa kazakazash, mangqtu iaku mathuaw maqaran numa maqitan tu Thau iaku.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為邵族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Thau is unique and something to be proud of.
